commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fee318daaf238e306ef413b946ca165fba0329a3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens plugin, schema, provider, Gateway, Codex, and SDK metadata handling so malformed or unreadable plugin-owned values are quarantined, diagnosed, skipped, or failed closed instead of crashing broad runtime paths.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking regression: source inspection shows any readable app/list branding, appMetadata, or labels are rewritten to null in the PR head. The broader fuzz-hardening behavior is covered by many synthetic tests in the PR body, but I did not run them in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This draft maintainer PR needs author cleanup for the metadata regression plus maintainer acceptance and exact-head proof for broad fail-closed upgrade behavior.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardened malformed plugin/tool/schema metadata boundaries so hostile or unreadable synthetic plugin data is quarantined, omitted, diagnosed, or treated as absent instead of crashing provider routing, runtime tool resolution, Gateway/MCP catalogs, Codex prompt/report paths, plugin SDK helpers, bundle MCP projection/materialization, Codex dynamic-tool bridge setup, plugin-tools MCP execution, and Plugin SDK host-hook/HTTP/provider-like/model-catalog/agent-harness/gateway-method/CLI-backend/embedding/text-transform registration.
- Kept fixtures synthetic: tests and notes use names like `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, `mockplugin_status`, `mockplugin_store`, and `fuzz_move_*`, not the reported third-party plugin name.
- Latest update guards Plugin SDK model catalog providers: provider ids, kind lists, and catalog hooks are now bounded/snapshotted, malformed providers diagnose and skip, and healthy sibling catalog providers still register with bound hooks.
- Recent updates also guard plugin text-transform registration, CLI backend registration, embedding-provider registration, gateway method registration, HTTP route registration, provider-like registrations, agent harness registration, and the strict OpenAI schema cache read path after rebasing across upstream cache changes.
- PR remains draft while this fuzzing/hardening lane continues.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas and hostile plugin/config/provider/manifest/native-approval/outbound-action/bundle-MCP/Codex dynamic-tool/Plugin-SDK metadata can crash, widen decisions, or bypass schema quarantine before normal OpenClaw diagnostics, setup, provider routing, gateway startup planning, Gateway catalog rendering, MCP loopback handling, approval forwarding, manifest/capability auth gating, provider payload conversion, prompt-cache setup, Codex reporting/instruction generation, plugin SDK normalization, plugin host-hook registration, HTTP route registration, provider-like registration, model catalog registration, agent harness registration, gateway method registration, CLI backend registration, embedding-provider registration, text-transform registration, agent-core tool lookup, cached plugin descriptor execution, static plugin tool registration, bundled capability runtime capture, bundle MCP catalog projection, bundle MCP catalog materialization, Codex dynamic-tool bridge setup, plugin-tools MCP execution, lifecycle/event host-hook registration, session scheduler/action host-hook registration, session extension/trusted policy host-hook registration, reload/node host/node invoke/security audit registration, service/gateway discovery service registration, or command registration completes.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`, local focused lint/format checks, local autoreview for recent behavioral slices, synthetic/private-name scrub, and AWS Crabbox Linux `pnpm check:changed` from the prior stack. Blacksmith Testbox was unavailable in this shell because `blacksmith auth login` is required.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- Pushed branch head `fee318daaf238e306ef413b946ca165fba0329a3`. The current stack was rebased onto `origin/main` `f1235477de3ec63dbfaa4f64aa3ca95895e88f80`.
- Latest model-catalog/provider-like focused format/diff checks: `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/model-catalog-registration.ts src/plugins/registry.provider-like.test.ts && git diff --check`.
- Latest model-catalog/provider-like focused lint: `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/model-catalog-registration.ts src/plugins/registry.provider-like.test.ts`.
- Latest model-catalog/provider-like focused Vitest proof after rebase: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/registry.provider-like.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
- Latest model-catalog local review: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`, which returned clean with no accepted/actionable findings.
- Previous text-transform/CLI-backend focused Vitest proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/registry.text-transforms.test.ts src/plugins/registry.cli-embedding.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
- Previous CLI/embedding/schema-cache focused Vitest proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/embedding-provider.contract.test.ts src/plugins/registry.cli-embedding.test.ts src/agents/openai-tool-schema.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
- Previous gateway-method focused proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/registry.gateway-method.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "gateway method"`.
- Previous broad AWS Crabbox changed gate from the earlier stack: `CRABBOX_AWS_REGION=us-east-1 CRABBOX_CAPACITY_REGIONS=us-east-1,us-east-2,us-west-2,eu-west-1,eu-central-1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "corepack pnpm check:changed"`.
- Changed-file, spec, and PR-body scrub for the reported plugin name and private path patterns.

Evidence after fix:

- Latest model-catalog focused format/diff checks passed.
- Latest model-catalog targeted oxlint passed.
- Latest model-catalog focused Vitest passed after rebase: 1 file, 10 tests passed.
- Model-catalog autoreview was clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported.
- Text-transform focused proof passed after its rebase: 2 files, 4 tests passed, with clean targeted lint/format and clean autoreview after revoked-proxy and proxy-RegExp findings were fixed.
- CLI-backend/embedding-provider focused proof passed after its rebase: 3 files, 11 tests passed, with clean targeted lint/format and clean autoreview.
- Gateway-method focused proof passed earlier after rebase: 2 files, 4 selected tests passed, 145 skipped by the `gateway method` filter, with clean targeted lint/format and clean autoreview.
- Prior focused schema/transport/registry Vitest passed: 6 files, 443 tests passed.
- Agent-harness autoreview reran clean with no accepted/actionable findings after several real compatibility findings were fixed.
- Provider-like focused proof passed earlier: 1 file, 9 tests passed, with clean targeted lint/format and clean autoreview after preserving provider instances.
- HTTP route focused proof passed earlier after rebase: 2 files, 48 tests passed, with clean targeted lint/format and clean autoreview.
- AWS Crabbox `check:changed` passed on provider `aws`, lease `cbx_4fdce7317314`, run `run_f0ee5dfc90be`, against base `07870dff45eb` before later drift rebases.
- Current branch diff, local spec, and PR body scrub pass for the reported plugin name and private paths.

Observed result after fix:

- Synthetic `fuzzplugin`/`mockplugin` fixtures with unreadable names, schemas, descriptions, approval fields, provider metadata, provider-like ids, model catalog provider fields/kinds/hooks, agent harness ids, gateway method names/scopes, CLI backend ids/config/text-transform fields, embedding provider ids/create fields, MCP catalog fields, cached runtime descriptors, Codex dynamic-tool descriptors, MCP argument preparers, Plugin SDK tool metadata, Plugin SDK Control UI descriptors, runtime lifecycle fields, agent event subscription fields, scheduler job fields, session action fields, session extension fields, trusted policy fields, reload prefix fields, node host command fields, node invoke policy fields, service fields, gateway discovery service fields, command fields, and HTTP route fields no longer crash the relevant projection/resolution/registration paths.
- Healthy siblings continue through materialization, descriptor caching, provider payload conversion, MCP/Gateway listing, agent-core validation, Codex dynamic-tool bridge registration, plugin-tools MCP execution, plugin host-hook registration, HTTP route registration, provider-like registration, model catalog registration, agent harness registration, gateway method registration, CLI backend registration, embedding-provider registration, and text-transform registration while malformed siblings are omitted, quarantined, or diagnosed.
- Model catalog registration now snapshots provider ids and kind arrays, rejects unreadable provider/kinds fields and invalid catalog hooks, avoids spreading arbitrary provider state, and binds healthy catalog hooks to the original receiver.
- Text-transform registration now snapshots plugin-owned arrays, rejects revoked proxy arrays and malformed replacement records, clones regex replacements before runtime use, and keeps healthy standalone/CLI backend text transforms registered.
- CLI backend registration now snapshots known top-level fields and avoids enumerating arbitrary plugin-owned backend state during registration.
- Embedding-provider registration now rejects malformed adapters before registry/global state capture and preserves class-backed adapter receivers for runtime creation.
- Gateway method registration rejects malformed method names, invalid handlers, unreadable scopes, and invalid scopes before registering unusable gateway handlers or descriptors.
- Agent harness registration exposes stable normalized metadata while preserving runtime method receivers for class-backed and plain-object harnesses.
- Strict OpenAI schema cache reads keep cache hits distinguishable from cache misses without relying on `undefined` as a sentinel.

What was not tested:

- Blacksmith Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was not run because `blacksmith` is not authenticated in this shell.
- AWS Crabbox `check:changed` was not rerun after the latest HTTP route, provider-like, agent-harness, strict schema cache-read, gateway-method, CLI-backend, embedding-provider, text-transform, and model-catalog slices; the previous broad pass was on the earlier stack, and the latest slices have focused proof, formatting, targeted oxlint, and autoreview where behavior changed.
- GitHub CI for the newest push is still running/pending.
- No live third-party plugin was referenced or exercised; tests intentionally use synthetic `fuzzplugin`/`mockplugin` fixtures.

## Current review state

- PR remains draft while this fuzzing/hardening lane continues.
- Latest pushed commit: `fee318daaf238e306ef413b946ca165fba0329a3` (`fix(plugins): guard model catalog registration`).
target.author: vincentkoc
